decision_summary: The later runtime-discipline benchmark run is materially better because it gives a stronger operator-facing explanation of failure-family coverage and auditability evidence.
rationale: The earlier run proved a single partial-chain rejection and a human-readable audit note. The later run still stays green on the positive path, but adds a second broken-chain family, machine-readable human-audit output, and stricter cost-threshold structure, so the claimed improvement is now visible in actual runtime-generated artifacts rather than only in synthetic fixtures.
recommendation: Accept this runtime-generated before/after pair as review-validity evidence and keep extending operator-facing benchmark evidence from real self-hosting traces.
target_audience: A framework operator deciding whether benchmark evidence quality improved across actual runtime runs.
expected_user_reaction: The later run should feel more credible because it shows broader failure coverage and more inspectable audit evidence.
artifact_change_recommendations: Keep exposing new failure families in runtime-generated benchmark runs.
Prefer machine-readable audit packets when claiming that auditability improved.
organization_change_recommendations: Require runtime-generated evidence when claiming benchmark quality improvement.
Continue treating benchmark output quality as a review target, not just a test artifact.
human_override_signal: Owner expectation: benchmark improvement claims should be backed by actual runtime-generated before/after evidence, not only synthetic fixtures.
